title: Soft-gated regime mixture for undercut-friendly vs overcut-friendly races
summary: Official F1 strategy explainers emphasize that undercut strength depends on degradation, tyre warm-up difficulty, low-grip tracks, and whether fresh tyres switch on immediately.
rationale: Official F1 strategy explainers emphasize that undercut strength depends on degradation, tyre warm-up difficulty, low-grip tracks, and whether fresh tyres switch on immediately. That implies one global model is averaging across distinct stop-timing regimes. A small mixture-of-experts can let one specialist learn high-deg undercut behavior and another learn low-deg/cool/warm-up-limited overcut behavior.
implementation_hint: Build 2-3 experts with the same base learner but different feature emphases, then blend them with a learned gate from `Compound`, wet/dry flag, `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, `RaceProgress`, `Race`, and race-level priors. Start with experts for `high-deg undercut`, `low-deg overcut/go-long`, and `wet/transition` rather than a large ensemble.
expected_effect: Should sharpen probabilities around tracks where identical tyre ages imply different stop incentives, improving edge cases the current single-rule service-window features smooth over.
risk: If the gate is too noisy, this becomes variance-heavy and may just repackage the same signal without real regime separation.
